name: Thorny broom, Ra Bridge, Hermitage of Lomos de Orios – circular
name_original: Achichuelo, Puente Ra, Ermita de Lomos de Orios – circular
region_intro: Jogging around Villoslada De Cameros offers a diverse landscape for runners. The region is characterized by mountainous terrain, including significant peaks like Pico Urbión, and features scenic reservoirs. Running routes often traverse varied elevations, providing both challenging ascents and more moderate paths. The area's natural beauty includes forests and open spaces, making it suitable for different running preferences. Best jogging routes around Villoslada De Cameros The most popular jogging route is Pico Urbión Loop from Laguna Negra, a difficult 8.3 miles (13.4 km) trail that takes 2 hours 37 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic mountain views. Another top favourite among local runners is González Lacasa Reservoir - El Rasillo Loop, a moderate 7.8 miles (12.5 km) path. This running route offers scenic views around the reservoir and through natural landscapes. Local runners also love the Thorny broom, Ra Bridge, Hermitage of Lomos de Orios – circular, a 4.6 miles (7.4 km) trail leading through varied terrain and past historical points of interest, often completed in about 1 hour. Jogging around Villoslada De Cameros is defined by mountain trails, reservoir paths, and forested sections. The network offers options for moderate and difficult running, catering to various fitness levels. The guide includes highly-rated routes that showcase the best of the region's landscape. More than 100 runners have used komoot to explore Villoslada De Cameros's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many running trails are available in Villoslada De Cameros?
A: There are currently 9 running routes documented on komoot for Villoslada De Cameros, offering a variety of experiences for runners.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on the running routes in Villoslada De Cameros?
A: The region is characterized by diverse terrain, including mountainous sections, paths around scenic reservoirs, and forested areas. You'll find routes with varied elevations, from challenging ascents to more moderate, undulating paths.

Q: Are there options for beginners or easy runs in Villoslada De Cameros?
A: While many routes in Villoslada De Cameros feature significant elevation, there are moderate options available. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990077859/thorny-broom-ra-bridge-hermitage-of-lomos-de-orios-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thorny broom, Ra Bridge, Hermitage of Lomos de Orios – circular&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 4.6-mile (7.4 km) trail that offers a less strenuous experience.

Q: Are there any circular running routes in the area?
A: Yes, many of the running routes in Villoslada De Cameros are circular, allowing you to start and finish at the same point. Examples include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990082907/gonzalez-lacasa-reservoir-el-rasillo-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;González Lacasa Reservoir - El Rasillo Loop&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990077859/thorny-broom-ra-bridge-hermitage-of-lomos-de-orios-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thorny broom, Ra Bridge, Hermitage of Lomos de Orios – circular&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What natural sights can I see while running in Villoslada De Cameros?
A: The running trails often pass by beautiful natural attractions. You can encounter stunning lakes and reservoirs like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3844856" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Laguna Larga&lt;/a&gt;, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2089062" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cebollera Lagoon&lt;/a&gt;, and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4100002" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;González Lacasa Reservoir&lt;/a&gt;. Some routes also offer views of mountain passes such as the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5449542" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Portillo of the Laguna Negra de Soria&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any historical or cultural landmarks along the running paths?
A: Yes, some routes incorporate historical points of interest. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990077859/thorny-broom-ra-bridge-hermitage-of-lomos-de-orios-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thorny broom, Ra Bridge, Hermitage of Lomos de Orios – circular&lt;/a&gt; trail passes by the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5323140" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita de Lomos de Orios&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3456725" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Puente Ra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Villoslada De Cameros?
A: Spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant running conditions with mild temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summer can be warm, especially at lower elevations, while winter may bring snow to higher mountain trails, making them more challenging or inaccessible.

Q: Are the running routes in Villoslada De Cameros suitable for families?
A: While many routes involve varied terrain and elevation, some moderate trails might be suitable for active families. It's best to check the difficulty and distance of individual routes to ensure they match your family's fitness level. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e990077859/thorny-broom-ra-bridge-hermitage-of-lomos-de-orios-circular" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Thorny broom, Ra Bridge, Hermitage of Lomos de Orios – circular&lt;/a&gt; is a good example of a moderate option.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails?
A: Generally, dogs are welcome on many trails in natural areas, but it's always advisable to keep them on a leash, especially in protected areas or near livestock. Please check local regulations for specific trails or nature parks you plan to visit.

Q: Are there any longer, more challenging running routes for experienced runners?
A: Yes, Villoslada De Cameros offers several difficult routes for seasoned runners. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20150351" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Olmo de El Rasillo – Peña Hincada Pass loop from El Rasillo de Cameros&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 31.7 km (19.7 miles) route with significant elevation gain, perfect for those seeking a demanding run.

Q: Where can I find parking for the running trails?
A: Specific parking information varies by trailhead. Many routes start from or pass through villages like Villoslada De Cameros or El Rasillo de Cameros, where public parking may be available. For routes starting in more remote areas, look for designated parking spots near trailheads or consult the komoot route details for specific starting points.

Q: Are there any viewpoints on the running trails?
A: Given the mountainous landscape, many running routes offer panoramic views. Trails that ascend to higher elevations or mountain passes, such as those near &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5321700" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peña Hincada Pass&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1064155512/pico-urbion-loop-from-laguna-negra" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico Urbión&lt;/a&gt;, are likely to provide stunning vistas of the surrounding region.
tips: Nice place to contact nature. It has many tables and barbecues next to the river and if it rains there is a shelter with tables inside. Perfect for the summer because there are many shady places.
Beautiful walk in the mountains of La Rioja. To be done at any time of the year.
Its name comes from "lumbos aureos", due to the golden color of the snowy hills at dawn.
